title: aliella latifolia
intro: Aliella latifolia, also known as the White Horehound, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its downy leaves and white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Aliella latifolia include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, while also possessing antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Aliella latifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to combat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
